changelog: **`gitmap list --update`** (alias `lu`, also `gitmap update list`) - Read-only table of every scanned repo whose local tag is behind the latest known remote release. Flags: `--json`, `--source release|import|git-tag`, `--limit`, `--stale-days`, `--only-behind`, `--path-only`. Exit 2 when the scan cache is missing.

**`gitmap update apply <repo>`** (alias `ua`) - Upgrade one scanned repo to its latest release tag. Flags: `--yes/-y`, `--dry-run`, `--tag`, `--strategy fetch-checkout|source-release`, `--stash`, `--json`. On failure enqueues a `PendingTask` of the new `Upgrade` type so `gitmap do-pending` can replay it.

**`gitmap update all`** (alias `uall`) - Iterate every row from `list --update` with a worker pool. Flags: `--yes/-y`, `--dry-run`, `--only`, `--parallel`, `--strategy`, `--stash`, `--stop-on-error`, `--json`. Exit 0 all ok, 1 partial, 2 nothing upgradable.

**`gitmap hd`** (alias for `gitmap help --dashboard`) - One-screen operator dashboard: installed vs latest gitmap version, scanned/upgradable/unknown repo counts, pending queue depth with the oldest task, last-scan timestamp, and the tail of completed tasks. `--json` for scripts, `--refresh` to re-probe first.
**`gitmap stats` Upgrades block** - Both text and JSON output gain a top-level `upgrades` section (`scanned`, `upToDate`, `upgradable`, `unknown`, `lastScanAt`).

**Post-scan hint** - `gitmap scan` prints `ℹ  N repo(s) have upgrades available. Run:  gitmap list --update` when the probe cache reports `upgradable > 0` and stderr is a TTY. Suppressed by `--quiet`.
**Pending task workflow** - New `TaskType` seed `Upgrade`. `SQLSeedTaskTypes` extended. `gitmap pending` and `gitmap do-pending` recognize and replay the new type via `gitmap update apply`.
**Helptext** - New embedded files `gitmap/helptext/list-update.md`, `update-apply.md`, `update-all.md`, `hd.md`. Cross-links added to `scan.md`, `stats.md`, `pending.md`, `do-pending.md`.
**Docs site** - `src/data/commands.ts` gains entries for `list --update`, `update apply`, `update all`, and `hd` with full usage, flags, examples, and see-also links.
